title: Electro-acoustic resonator and method of forming thereof
abstract: An electro-acoustic resonator comprises a piezoelectric substrate (310) on which an electrode structure is disposed. The electrode structure comprises a metal layer (535) of aluminum and copper, a barrier layer (450) forming a barrier against the diffusion of copper and another metal layer (421) disposed on the barrier layer comprising aluminum. An AlCu intermetallic phase formed after an anneal is restricted to the portion beneath the barrier layer (450) so that Galvano-corrosion of the electrode structure is avoided.
claims:
Claims Claims : 1. An electro-acoustic resonator, comprising: a substrate (310), the substrate having piezoelectric properties ; an electrode structure (421, 412) disposed on the substrate, the electrode structure comprising: a metal layer (535) , the metal layer comprising aluminum and copper; a barrier layer (450) disposed on the metal layer to form a barrier against the diffusion of copper; and another metal layer (421) disposed on the barrier layer, the other metal layer comprising aluminum. 2. The electro-acoustic resonator of claim 1, wherein the metal layer (535) comprises grains of an intermetallic phase comprising aluminum and copper. 3. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the barrier layer (450) comprises a metal or a metal nitride . 4. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the barrier layer (450) comprises at least one of titanium, chromium, cobalt, tantal, tungsten, a nitride of one of titanium, tantal and tungsten. 5. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the thickness of the metal layer (535) is at least half of the thickness of the other metal layer (421) . 6. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein, within the metal layer (535) , the mass of copper is equal to the mass of aluminum. 7. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the thickness of the metal layer (635) is larger than the thickness of the other metal layer (641), wherein the thickness of the other metal layer (641) is in the range of 15 nm to 30 nm, preferably in the range of 20 nm to 25 nm. 8. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the substrate (310) comprises lithium tantalate or lithium niobate. 9. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 8, further comprising a seed layer (430, 630) disposed between the substrate (310) and the metal layer (535), the seed layer comprising at least one of titanium and chromium. 10. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 8, further comprising a seed layer (430, 630) disposed between the substrate (310) and the metal layer (535), the seed layer comprising a metal, wherein the metal has a hardness of at least 1 Gigapascal. 11. The electro-acoustic resonator of claim 10, wherein the seed layer (430, 630) comprises a metal having one of a body centered cubic crystal structure and a hexagonal closed packed crystal structure. 12. The electro-acoustic resonator of claim 10 or 11, wherein the seed layer (430, 630) comprises a metal selected from the 

group consisting of chromium, cobalt, niobium, molybdenum and tungsten . 13. The electro-acoustic resonator of any of claims 1 to 12, wherein the electro-acoustic resonator is a surface acoustic wave resonator and the electrode structure (421, 412) forms an interdigital transducer arrangement (110, 120). 14. A method of forming an electro-acoustic resonator, comprising the steps of: providing a substrate (310) made of a piezoelectric material; forming a copper layer (431) on the piezoelectric material; forming an aluminium layer (440) on the copper layer (431); forming a barrier layer (450) on the aluminum layer (440), the barrier layer (450) configured to form a barrier against the diffusion of copper; forming another aluminum (421) layer on the barrier layer (450) ; and annealing the formed structure to enable a diffusion of copper from the copper layer (431) into the aluminum layer (440) . 15. The method according to claim 14, comprising forming the aluminum layer (440) with the same mass as the copper layer (431) and forming a barrier layer (450) comprising at least one of titanium, chromium, cobalt, tantal, tungsten, a nitride of one of titanium, tantal and tungsten.
